questions.original_question: As of 2023, what is Zeekr's product portfolio?
questions.answer:
As of 2023, Zeekr's product portfolio includes several notable models. The Zeekr 001, a premium crossover shooting brake, was the first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV) with over 1,000 kilometers CLTC range, launched in April 2021. In October 2023, Zeekr introduced the Zeekr 001 FR, a high-performance variant with advanced features like a lightweight carbon fiber body and an 800V Qilin battery. The Zeekr 009, a luxury six-seater MPV, was launched in November 2022, offering a premium mobility experience. Additionally, the Zeekr X, a compact SUV, was released in April 2023, featuring spacious design and advanced technology. These models highlight Zeekr's commitment to innovation and performance in the electric vehicle market.

The information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

rag_info.chunk_content: Zeekr 001, Zeekr's first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV), is a premium crossover shooting brake developed with proprietary research and development (R&D), deep market insights, and the Sustainable Experience Architecture (SEA) platform. Launched in April 2021 with deliveries beginning in October 2021, the Zeekr 001 was the first mass-produced BEV with over $1,000 kilometers CLTC range. In October 2023, Zeekr introduced the Zeekr 001 FR, a high-performance variant featuring a lightweight carbon fiber body, 800V Qilin battery, and quad e-drive system, achieving 2.02 seconds 0-100 kilometers per hour acceleration and a 280 kilometers per hour top speed. In 2024, Zeekr launched upgraded versions of the Zeekr 001 with Zeekr Intelligent Driving 2.0 and significant performance, safety, and intelligence upgrades.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology is a fast-growing BEV technology company. Through developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, Lotus Technology aspires to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Since its inception, Lotus Technology has focused on innovation in BEV architecture, hardware, software, and the application of new technologies. Lotus Technology's efforts are backed by strong in-house R&D capabilities, a deep understanding of products, high operational flexibility, and a flat, efficient organizational structure. Together, these features enable fast product development, launch, and iteration, as well as a series of customer-oriented products and go-to-market strategies. Thus, Lotus Technology is able to rapidly expand even with a limited operating history. Lotus Technology strategically spearheaded the premium intelligent BEV market with unique positioning, featuring a strong sense of technology, in-house R&D capabilities, stylish design, high-caliber performance, and a premium user experience. Lotus Technology's current product portfolio primarily includes ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. ZEEKR 001. With an unwavering commitment to its mission, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 in April 2021, a five-seater, cross-over hatchback vehicle model with superior performance and functionality. Targeting the premium BEV market, ZEEKR 001 is Lotus Technology's first vehicle model and the world’s first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 001 is also the first mass-produced BEV model with over $1,000 km CLTC range, according to Frost & Sullivan. Lotus Technology began the delivery of ZEEKR 001 in October 2021.

title: CEVT 
summary: Zeekr Technology Europe AB, also known as CEVT, is a Swedish research and development center established in 2013, focusing on automotive innovation for international markets, particularly in Europe and the United States. CEVT specializes in next-generation mobility solutions, including robotaxis and passenger battery electric vehicles (BEVs) that comply with local standards. The center is also developing advanced digital tools such as data analytics, smart cockpits, and fleet operations, while emphasizing user-centric systems that integrate in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities. 

One of CEVT's key projects is the SEA-M platform, an enhanced version of its SEA platform designed to support ZEEKR's future BEVs. This platform will feature autonomous driving capabilities and smart mobility options, promising a superior driving experience with innovative designs like a B-pillar-less structure and advanced air conditioning technologies. As of December 31, 2023, CEVT employed 715 individuals, primarily focused on mobility technologies and product development.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 23.   Segment reporting (Continued) 
summary: CEVT (China Euro Vehicle Technology) operates as a research and development entity in Sweden, focusing on the automotive sector and catering primarily to Chinese partners. The group's performance is evaluated through its reportable segments—ZEEKR, Viridi, and CEVT—by analyzing revenues and segment profits, which mainly derive from vehicle and battery sales, as well as R&D services. 

Financial results for the fiscal years ending December 31 from 2021 to 2023 show significant growth across all segments. In 2021, ZEEKR reported revenues of RMB 1,821,130 with a profit of RMB 76,567, while Viridi and CEVT generated revenues of RMB 2,521,951 (profit: RMB 40,427) and RMB 2,760,362 (profit: RMB 952,911), respectively. The consolidated total for that year was RMB 6,527,518 in revenues and RMB 1,038,169 in profit. By 2022, ZEEKR's revenues surged to RMB 20,630,041 (profit: RMB 1,042,599), Viridi's to RMB 12,799,124 (profit: RMB 1,170,415), and CEVT's to RMB 2,439,927 (profit: RMB 480,982), leading to consolidated revenues of RMB 31,899,448 and profits of RMB 2,472,050. In 2023, ZEEKR's revenues escalated further to RMB 35,614,648 (profit: RMB 5,791,938), Viridi's to RMB 18,203,200 (profit: RMB 954,215), and CEVT's to RMB 2,259,127 (profit: RMB 265,091), culminating in total revenues of RMB 51,672,618 and a net profit of RMB 6,850,530.

The asset performance also reflects growth, with ZEEKR's total assets increasing from RMB 7,748,554 in 2021 to RMB 16,746,231 in 2023, while Viridi's assets rose from RMB 4,060,704 to RMB 12,058,165. CEVT's assets, however, decreased from RMB 3,759,074 to RMB 3,189,275 over the same period. The consolidated total assets grew from RMB 11,939,932 in 2021 to RMB 27,117,500 in 2023.

A reconciliation of segment profits to the Group's overall financial results indicates a significant increase in gross profits from RMB 1,038,169 in 2021 to RMB 6,850,530 in 2023, alongside rising operational costs and losses before income tax, which expanded from RMB (4,517,404) to RMB (8,288,920). 

Geographically, the majority of revenues were generated from China, which contributed RMB 5,683,682 in 2021, RMB 28,848,370 in 2022, and RMB 45,975,347 in 2023. Sweden's contributions were notably smaller, with revenues of RMB 619,908 in 2021, rising to RMB 777,989 in 2022, but declining to RMB 706,906 in 2023. The data underscores China's dominance as a revenue source, with no other regions surpassing 10% of total revenues during the reported years.

Overall, the summary illustrates CEVT's strategic focus on innovation and development in the automotive sector, showcasing substantial growth in revenues and profits while highlighting the importance of geographical markets in driving financial performance.

title: Zeekr Tech EU 
summary: Zeekr Technology Europe AB (Zeekr Tech EU), established in 2013 in Sweden, serves as a research and development hub focused on creating innovative products for international markets. The organization specializes in next-generation mobility solutions, including robotaxis and battery electric vehicles (BEVs) tailored to local standards, alongside digital offerings like data analytics and smart cockpit functions. A significant focus is placed on advancing BEV technologies that prioritize user-centric systems, integrating in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities with centralized architecture for improved user experiences.

Central to Zeekr Tech EU's initiatives is the SEA-M platform, an evolution of the SEA architecture designed to support autonomous driving and smart mobility. This platform aims to deliver versatile mobility options, exceptional driving experiences, and long-lasting vehicles that can be continuously upgraded. BEVs developed on this platform, such as the Zeekr Mix and Waymo RT robotaxi, feature innovative designs, including wide door openings and flexible interiors. By the end of 2024, the company employed 733 individuals, focusing on mobility technologies, digital solutions, and future product development.

title: COMBINED AND CONSOLIDATED STATEMENTS OF OPERATIONS FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2022, 2023 AND 2024 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: The consolidated statements of operations for Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2022, 2023, and 2024, illustrate the company's evolving financial landscape. Key metrics such as net revenues, costs, gross profit, operating expenses, and net losses are presented in RMB for the first two years and in USD for 2024.

Notable trends include a substantial increase in net revenues, which rose from RMB 31.9 billion in 2022 to RMB 75.9 billion in 2024, primarily driven by vehicle sales. Correspondingly, the cost of revenues also escalated, climbing from RMB 29.4 billion to RMB 63.5 billion over the same period, reflecting heightened expenses related to vehicle production and components. Despite these rising costs, gross profit improved significantly, increasing from RMB 2.5 billion in 2022 to RMB 12.4 billion in 2024.

Operating expenses grew from RMB 9.6 billion to RMB 18.9 billion, fueled by investments in research and development as well as selling, general, and administrative costs. Importantly, the net loss decreased from RMB 7.7 billion in 2022 to RMB 5.8 billion in 2024, indicating a positive shift in financial performance. The net loss per share showed a similar trend, with figures of RMB (3.97) for 2022, RMB (4.17) for 2023, and RMB (2.73) for 2024.

In summary, while Zeekr continues to face operating losses, the financial statements reveal a robust growth trajectory in revenues and gross profit margins, underscoring the company's commitment to its growth and development initiatives.

title: OUR VEHICLES 
summary: ZEEKR is strategically positioned in the premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, focusing on the design, engineering, and sale of advanced electric vehicles that enhance user experience and drivability. Leveraging its strong research and development capabilities and synergies with Geely Group, ZEEKR aims to tap into China's burgeoning premium BEV market, which is projected to grow significantly from 666,400 units in 2024 to 2,607,600 units by 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan.

Since its inception, ZEEKR has launched several models, starting with the ZEEKR 001 in 2021, followed by the upgraded 2024 model, the ZEEKR 009, and the compact SUV ZEEKR X. The company has also expanded its offerings with the ZEEKR 001 FR and an upscale sedan, reflecting its commitment to catering to diverse customer needs. As of December 31, 2023, ZEEKR has delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles, primarily in China, marking one of the fastest growth rates in the premium BEV segment.

The monthly delivery data from June 2023 to March 2024 shows a consistent upward trend, with March 2024 witnessing 13,012 units delivered. This growth is indicative of rising consumer demand and ZEEKR's operational capabilities, positioning the company as a significant player in the global market. ZEEKR has also begun deliveries in Europe and plans to supply vehicles for the Waymo One Fleet in the U.S., highlighting its ambitions for international expansion. Overall, ZEEKR's trajectory reflects a robust commitment to innovation and market growth in the premium electric vehicle sector.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a fast-growing BEV technology company. Through developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, Lotus Technology aspires to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Since its inception, Lotus Technology has focused on innovation in BEV architecture, hardware, software, and application of new technologies. Lotus Technology's efforts are backed by strong in-house R&D capabilities, a deep understanding of products, high operational flexibility, and a flat, efficient organizational structure. Together, these features enable fast product development, launch, and iteration, as well as a series of customer-oriented products and go-to-market strategies. Thus, Lotus Technology is able to rapidly expand even with a limited operating history. Lotus Technology strategically spearheaded the premium intelligent BEV market with unique positioning, featuring a strong sense of technology, in-house R&D capabilities, stylish design, high-caliber performance, and a premium user experience. Lotus Technology's current product portfolio primarily includes ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. ZEEKR 001. With an unwavering commitment to its mission, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 in April 2021, a five-seater, cross-over hatchback vehicle model with superior performance and functionality. Targeting the premium BEV market, ZEEKR 001 is Lotus Technology's first vehicle model and the world’s first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 001 is also the first mass-produced BEV model with over $1,000 km CLTC range, according to Frost & Sullivan. Lotus Technology began the delivery of ZEEKR 001 in October 2021.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging leader in the battery electric vehicle (BEV) sector, dedicated to driving the electrification and innovation of the automotive industry through the development of premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions. Since its inception, ZEEKR has focused on advancing BEV architecture, hardware, software, and the integration of new technologies, supported by strong in-house research and development (R&D) capabilities and an efficient organizational structure. This approach enables rapid product development and effective market strategies, facilitating swift expansion despite its relatively short operating history.

The company has made notable progress with its innovative vehicle lineup, which includes the ZEEKR 001, the world's first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, launched in April 2021, and the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, introduced in November 2022. The ZEEKR 001 has achieved significant sales milestones, including delivering over 10,000 units within four months of its launch and surpassing 10,000 monthly deliveries in October 2022. By the end of 2023, ZEEKR had delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles, primarily in China, positioning it as a rapidly growing player in the premium BEV market.

ZEEKR's development is underpinned by the SEA platform, an open-source modular architecture owned by Geely Holding, which allows for a diverse range of vehicle types and rapid integration of advanced technologies. The company benefits from Geely's extensive industry experience, innovative engineering, and strong supply chain management, which enhance product reliability and consumer interest.

In addition to its vehicle offerings, ZEEKR emphasizes a customer-centric approach, utilizing a direct-to-consumer sales model and a robust charging network to enhance the user experience. The company has formed strategic partnerships with leading firms like Mobileye and Waymo to advance autonomous driving technologies and expand its global reach.

The BEV market is poised for significant growth, particularly in China and Europe, with projections indicating a substantial increase in sales by 2028. ZEEKR is well-positioned to capitalize on these opportunities, focusing on next-generation mobility solutions that combine luxury, performance, and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In February 2024, Lotus Technology released an upgraded model of ZEEKR 001, or ZEEKR 001 (2024 model). Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 (2024 model) in March 2024. In October 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 FR, its cross-over hatchback vehicle model based on ZEEKR 001. Featuring unique exterior and interior design and proprietary technologies, ZEEKR 001 FR is designed to offer outstanding vehicle performance with various driving modes. Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 FR in November 2023. • ZEEKR 009. In November 2022, Lotus Technology launched its second model, ZEEKR 009, a luxury six-seater MPV model providing a comfortable, ultra-luxury mobility experience for both families and business uses. ZEEKR 009 is the world’s first premium MPV based on a pure-electric platform, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 009 has enjoyed wide popularity since launch, and Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 009 to its customers in January 2023. In April 2024, Lotus Technology launched ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury version of ZEEKR 009 featuring enhanced safety, privacy, and intelligence. Lotus Technology also released ZEEKR MIX, its MPV model, in the same month. • ZEEKR X. In April 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR X, its compact SUV model featuring spacious interior design, advanced technology, and superior driving performance. Lotus Technology began to deliver ZEEKR X in June 2023. • ZEEKR Upscale Sedan Model. In November 2023, Lotus Technology launched its first upscale sedan model targeting tech-savvy adults and families. Powered by 800V architecture and multi-link suspension.

This consistent growth illustrates ZEEKR's successful penetration into the premium electric vehicle market and its increasing consumer acceptance, particularly for the ZEEKR 001 model. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a fast-growing BEV technology company. Through developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, Lotus Technology aspires to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Since its inception, Lotus Technology has focused on innovation in BEV architecture, hardware, software, and the application of new technologies. Lotus Technology's efforts are backed by strong in-house R&D capabilities, a deep understanding of products, high operational flexibility, and a flat, efficient organizational structure. Together, these features enable fast product development, launch, and iteration, as well as a series of customer-oriented products and go-to-market strategies. Thus, Lotus Technology is able to rapidly expand even with a limited operating history. Lotus Technology strategically spearheaded the premium intelligent BEV market with unique positioning, featuring a strong sense of technology, in-house R&D capabilities, stylish design, high-caliber performance, and a premium user experience. Lotus Technology's current product portfolio primarily includes ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. ZEEKR 001. With an unwavering commitment to its mission, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 in April 2021, a five-seater, cross-over hatchback vehicle model with superior performance and functionality. Targeting the premium BEV market, ZEEKR 001 is Lotus Technology's first vehicle model and the world’s first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 001 is also the first mass-produced BEV model with over $1,000 km CLTC range, according to Frost & Sullivan. Lotus Technology began the delivery of ZEEKR 001 in October 2021.

title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging leader in the battery electric vehicle (BEV) sector, focusing on premium models and innovative technology solutions. With a strong emphasis on in-house research and development, the company has established a flexible organizational structure that enables rapid product development and market entry. Since its inception, ZEEKR has launched several notable models, including the ZEEKR 001, recognized as the world's first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, and the ZEEKR 009, a luxury MPV. The company has achieved impressive sales milestones, delivering over 196,000 vehicles by the end of 2023, with significant growth in both the Chinese and European markets.

ZEEKR leverages the SEA platform from Geely Group, which provides a modular architecture for various vehicle types and supports advanced technologies. The company is committed to enhancing its vehicles with cutting-edge features, including autonomous driving capabilities and an intelligent cockpit experience. ZEEKR's robust supply chain and manufacturing capabilities, coupled with strategic partnerships with industry leaders like Mobileye and CATL, further bolster its competitive edge.

The BEV market is poised for substantial growth, particularly in China and Europe, driven by technological advancements and favorable regulations. ZEEKR aims to capitalize on this trend by expanding its product lineup and enhancing customer engagement through a direct-to-consumer sales model and a comprehensive charging network. The company's vision is to redefine premium mobility with innovative, high-performance electric vehicles that cater to the evolving needs of consumers.

Featuring unique exterior and interior design and proprietary technologies, ZEEKR 001 FR is designed to offer outstanding vehicle performance with various driving modes. Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 FR in November 2023. • ZEEKR 009. ZEEKR 009 has enjoyed wide popularity since launch, and Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 009 to its customers in January 2023. In April 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR X, its compact SUV model featuring spacious interior design, advanced technology, and superior driving performance. Lotus Technology began to deliver ZEEKR X in June 2023. • ZEEKR Upscale Sedan Model. In November 2023, Lotus Technology launched its first upscale sedan model targeting tech-savvy adults and families. Powered by 800V architecture and multi-link suspension.

title: CONDENSED STATEMENTS OF OPERATIONS AND COMPREHENSIVE LOSS FOR THE YEAR ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2021, 2022 AND 2023 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: The financial performance of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited from 2021 to 2023 reflects significant fluctuations in its operations and losses. Over these years, the company reported a loss from operations that escalated from $1,800 thousand in 2021 to $11,237 thousand in 2022, before decreasing to $1,583 thousand in 2023. Interest income initially rose from $2,240 thousand in 2021 to $6,268 thousand in 2022, then fell to $3,593 thousand in 2023. 

Other income transitioned from a loss of $152 thousand in 2021 to a gain of $1,826 thousand in 2022, reaching a peak of $54,782 thousand in the same year, before settling at $7,716 thousand in 2023. Income before tax showed a similar trend, increasing from $2,088 thousand in 2021 to $6,294 thousand in 2022, and hitting $69,058 thousand in 2022, before dropping to $9,726 thousand in 2023.

The company faced substantial equity losses from subsidiaries, which began at $4,364,657 thousand in 2021, worsened to $7,940,073 thousand in 2022, peaked at $8,416,038 thousand in 2022, and then significantly decreased to $1,185,374 thousand in 2023. Consequently, net losses were reported at $4,362,569 thousand in 2021, $7,933,779 thousand in 2022, and $8,346,980 thousand in 2022, before improving to $1,175,648 thousand in 2023. The comprehensive loss followed a similar pattern, starting at $4,465,974 thousand in 2021, decreasing to $7,919,223 thousand in 2022, and ultimately reaching $1,168,639 thousand in 2023.

The financial data has been reviewed for any subsequent events up to March 20, 2024, with no adjustments or disclosures required.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology began as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017. Lotus Technology conducts its business primarily through the following entities: (i) ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. (“ZEEKR Shanghai”), (ii) ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. (“ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay”), (iii) Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. (“Ningbo Viridi”) and (iv) Zeekr Technology Europe AB (“CEVT”). Under the leadership of the co-founders, Mr. Shufu Li, Mr. Conghui An, Mr. Donghui Li, and Mr. Shengyue Gui, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology was incorporated as an exempted company with limited liability in March 2021 under the law of the Cayman Islands to act as the holding company. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology is seeking to list separately from Geely Auto because of its different brand positioning and its operational, management, and financial independence. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology has an equity story built around its premium brand, product portfolio, and future plans that is better served by operating independently and seeking a separate listing, which the company believes will allow it to establish its own profile and attract different investors. In April 2021, ZEEKR Innovation, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology, was incorporated under the laws of the British Virgin Islands. In the same period, ZEEKR Technology, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Innovation, was incorporated under the laws of Hong Kong. In April 2021, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology announced the launch of its first BEV model, ZEEKR 001, and started delivery from October 2021.

title: Our Corporate History 
summary: ZEEKR originated as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017 and has since evolved into a distinct entity with a focus on premium electric vehicles. Established as ZEEKR Intelligent Technology in March 2021 in the Cayman Islands, the company was co-founded by Mr. Shufu Li and others to pursue operational and financial independence from Geely Auto, allowing it to cultivate its own brand identity and attract diverse investors. The organization operates through several subsidiaries, including ZEEKR Automobile and ZEEKR Technology, and has made significant acquisitions to enhance its capabilities, such as acquiring ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay and CEVT from Geely Holding.

ZEEKR launched its first battery electric vehicle, the ZEEKR 001, in April 2021, followed by the ZEEKR 009 in November 2022 and the ZEEKR X compact SUV in April 2023. The company continues to expand its product lineup, introducing models like the ZEEKR 001 FR in October 2023 and an upscale sedan in January 2024. In April 2024, ZEEKR unveiled the ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury variant, and the ZEEKR MIX, a multi-purpose vehicle. This strategic focus on premium offerings and innovative designs positions ZEEKR as a competitive player in the electric vehicle market.

summary: The inventory summary for Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited over the years 2021 to 2023 reveals significant growth in inventory assets. In 2021, the total inventory was RMB 1,214,080 thousand, consisting of RMB 375,837 thousand in raw materials, RMB 34,785 thousand in work in progress, and RMB 803,458 thousand in finished products. The upward trend continued into 2023, with total inventory reaching RMB 5,228,689 thousand. This year saw a notable rise in finished products (RMB 4,455,479 thousand), despite a decrease in raw materials (RMB 733,069 thousand) and a slight decline in work in progress (RMB 40,142 thousand). Overall, the data illustrates Zeekr's significant expansion in inventory and operational capacity over the three-year period.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: 5. Lotus Technology notes that the company purchases vehicles for resale from Geely Holding at a price made up of the purchase cost of direct materials and a pre-agreed markup. Please describe the manufacturer’s warranty provided by Geely Holding for these vehicles and to what extent the manufacturer’s warranties have an impact on Lotus Technology's accounting for warranty expenses, if material. In response to the Staff’s comment, Lotus Technology has revised the disclosure on page 90 of the Revised Draft Registration Statement. Lotus Technology respectfully advises the Staff that under the cooperation framework agreement between Lotus Technology and Geely Holding, ZEEKR Factory provides an assurance-type product warranty for all product quality problems caused by its manufacturing, with a warranty period no less than what Lotus Technology offers to its customers. In the event where the product defect is caused by raw material suppliers, Lotus Technology is entitled to seek damages from ZEEKR Factory, which in turn could seek damages from the responsible suppliers. Lotus Technology further advises the Staff that when estimating warranties, the accrual is established based on estimates of the nature, frequency, and average costs of future claims, net of estimated warranty cost recoveries, to the extent that is estimable and probable.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cooperation Framework Agreement, page 89 
summary: The summary describes the vehicle purchasing process from Geely Holding, which involves acquiring vehicles at a cost that includes direct material expenses plus a predetermined markup. It also highlights the manufacturer's warranty provided by Geely Holding, specifically an assurance-type product warranty for manufacturing-related quality issues, which aligns with the Company's customer offerings. The Company has updated its disclosure in the Revised Draft Registration Statement to reflect these details. Additionally, it addresses the accounting implications of warranty expenses, noting that warranty accruals are calculated based on estimates of future claims, taking into account the nature, frequency, and average costs, as well as any potential recoveries from raw material suppliers for defects.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Typically, customers would expect an original equipment manufacturer (OEM) to frequently offer and improve vehicle models. However, Lotus Technology only had one mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV) in the market before the end of 2022, and the company's business substantially depended on the sales and success of the ZEEKR 001. Since Lotus Technology's business will depend on a limited number of BEV models for the foreseeable future and the product portfolio may further evolve based on market demand, the sales volume could be materially and adversely affected if a particular model is not well received by the market. The existing models may also face fluctuations in delivery volume and cannot deliver as much as historical levels. The change in product mix may also cause the delivery volume and/or average selling price to change. This could have a material adverse effect on Lotus Technology's business, prospects, financial condition, and operating results. Going forward, Lotus Technology plans to launch more new models to enrich its product portfolio and periodically introduce new versions of existing vehicle models. However, Lotus Technology might experience delays in the launch of its new products and services. Therefore, Lotus Technology's future sales may be adversely affected to the extent its battery electric vehicles do not meet consumer expectations in terms of product variety or upgrade cycles, or cannot be produced pursuant to expected timelines, costs, or volume targets.

metadata.title_summary:
title: We expect to mainly generate revenues from the sales of a limited number of BEV models. 
summary: The company recognizes that customers expect regular updates and improvements from original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) regarding vehicle models. However, by the end of 2022, it had only one mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV), the ZEEKR 001, which has a significant impact on its business performance. This reliance on a limited BEV lineup poses risks, as the success of these models is critical for sales; underperformance of any model could lead to decreased sales volume. Additionally, fluctuations in delivery volumes and changes in the product mix may affect both delivery rates and average selling prices, potentially harming the company's financial health and overall performance.
